findings.description: logger.info logs the full card PAN (card_number) and CVV in cleartext, a direct PCI-DSS violation (CWE-532): CVV must never be persisted in any form and PAN must be masked; any log reader, log shipper, or aggregator now holds live card credentials.

charge_order performs a card charge keyed solely on a caller-supplied order_id with no ownership/authorization check (CWE-862/CWE-639 IDOR); if this public method is reachable directly, an attacker can charge arbitrary orders, though auth may legitimately live in an out-of-scope web layer.

refund flips an order to 'refunded' based only on a caller-supplied order_id with no authorization or actor check (CWE-862/CWE-639 IDOR), enabling unauthorized refunds of arbitrary orders if the method is directly reachable.

monthly_revenue_report exposes aggregate financial data (revenue, order/item counts) with no authorization gate (CWE-862); sensitive business data is returned to any caller able to invoke the entry point.

No exception handling/rollback around the post-charge DB UPDATE+commit (CWE-460, OWASP A10): if the card is charged successfully but the UPDATE or commit then fails, money is captured while the order stays unpaid and untracked, with no reconciliation path.

Transaction id derived from non-cryptographic, per-process-randomized Python hash() (CWE-330) yields weak, non-unique, predictable-within-process ids; flagged as low because this is explicitly an elided-SDK fixture stub, not production charge logic.

api_key retained as a plaintext instance attribute with no scrubbing/secret wrapper (CWE-256/CWE-316); minor in isolation since in-memory secrets are largely unavoidable and no leak path exists in this file.

discount_pct is unbounded (no 0-100 clamp) and money is computed in floats (unit_price: float, lines 56-60/73/119-121); a discount >100 yields negative totals and float rounding drift, but this is a logic/correctness concern deferred to the logic critic, and discount/price originate from the DB rather than direct user input here.
